name: Alpine Carbon 130
shortDescription: Lightweight carbon touring boot for committed ascents.
description: The Alpine Carbon 130 is built for the alpinist who refuses to compromise at either end of the day. A full-carbon cuff sheds 180g over conventional constructions, while the 130-flex index holds hard snow edges without deflection. The Grilamid shell is precision-injected with a 98mm last, accommodating a performance-fit liner with heat-moldable zones. Walk mode delivers 18° of ramp angle for clean skinning posture across sustained vertical. Designed for guide-pace objectives where every gram is justified and every descent deserves the full boot.

name: Meridian Tour 110
shortDescription: Versatile touring boot balancing comfort and precision.
description: The Meridian Tour 110 sits at the crossroads of all-day comfort and technical precision â€” the boot for skiers who do it all. A 110-flex Grilamid shell pairs with a 100mm last for a fit that accommodates a performance liner without sacrificing blood flow on multi-hour approaches. The walk mechanism is seamless to engage one-handed with a gloved grip, and the rockered rubber outsole grips approach terrain with confidence. This is the quiver-of-one touring boot: capable enough for steep couloirs, comfortable enough for dawn-to-dark ridge traverses.

name: Summit Race 140
shortDescription: Race-tuned alpine boot for podium-level performance.
description: The Summit Race 140 is a sanctioned race boot adapted for free skiing. The 140-flex monocoque shell eliminates the cuff-to-shell interface entirely, transmitting edge pressure with zero latency. The 96mm last demands precision fitting â€” we recommend a shell-fit appointment â€” and rewards it with a locked-in feel that competitive skiers describe as an extension of the foot. Three macro-adjustable buckles use aircraft-grade aluminum bails. No walk mode. No compromise. For the skier who has already made peace with that.

name: Freeride Pro 100
shortDescription: Wide-last freeride boot for big-mountain and powder terrain.
description: The Freeride Pro 100 is designed for the skier who lives in the sidecountry and charges deep snow without apology. A 102mm last accommodates a beefier liner â€” the Primaloft-insulated heat-moldable unit included â€” for warmth on cold powder days and a plush fit in thick socks. The 100-flex shell provides enough forgiveness to absorb variable snow conditions while still initiating turns with authority. An oversized walk-mode lever works in mitts. The directional sole lugs clear snow aggressively for bootpacking approaches. This is the boot for the skier whose objectives start where the groomer ends.

name: Piste Master 90
shortDescription: Confident all-mountain boot for intermediate to advanced skiers.
description: The Piste Master 90 is the boot for skiers who have graduated from rentals and know what they want: a proper fit, reliable performance across all conditions, and the confidence to push into steeper terrain. The 90-flex shell strikes the sweet spot between forgiveness and response. A 101mm last fits a mid-width foot in a performance liner without pressure points. Four micro-adjustable buckles and a power strap fine-tune fit across a full day of skiing. This is the boot you grow into â€” and grow with â€” as your skiing progresses.

name: Intro Comfort 75
shortDescription: Forgiving entry-level boot for new and returning skiers.
description: The Intro Comfort 75 makes your first days on snowâ€”or your return after years awayâ€”as straightforward as possible. The 75-flex shell forgives technical imperfections and reduces leg fatigue, while the wide 104mm last accommodates most foot shapes without pinching. An easy-entry design with a rear-spoiler latch lets you step in and out without gymnastics. The three-buckle system is color-coded for intuitive adjustment. Warm-climate insulation in the liner keeps circulation comfortable through long lesson days. No compromises in safety or build quality â€” just a friendly, confidence-building fit.
